INTELLIGENCE AUGMENTATION
A Beginner’s Course
Course Outline and Scaffold
7 Units · 4 Thematic Clusters · Beginner Level
Course Overview
Intelligence Augmentation is the practice of using tools — especially AI tools — to extend, enhance, and accelerate our natural cognitive abilities. Rather than asking whether artificial intelligence will replace human thinking, this course reframes the question: how do we use AI to think better, learn faster, and make clearer decisions?
This course is designed for beginners. No prior experience with AI tools is required. The only prerequisite is curiosity about how your own mind works and how it might work better.
The course is organized into seven units across four thematic clusters: Foundation, Framework, Practice, and Integration. Each unit builds directly on what came before. The sequence matters — students who skip ahead to the tools before understanding the underlying framework tend to use those tools as substitutes for thinking rather than extensions of it.
How to Use This Course
Units 01 and 02 are conceptual. Resist the urge to introduce AI tools in this phase. Students need to understand what they are augmenting before they augment it.
